findings.message: The CLI policy assigns adapter construction/DI to `apps/cli/` and explicitly exempts adapter construction from the `usecase bypass` category. In the current architecture, `cli` may depend only on `cli_composition`, and `cli_composition` owns domain/usecase/infrastructure wiring, so this briefing would tell reviewers not to report direct infrastructure/domain construction in CLI and overlaps the `cli_composition` briefing. Reframe CLI as thin arg parsing/output/ExitCode mapping that delegates wiring to `cli_composition`, and make direct domain/infrastructure construction in CLI reportable.

The CLI policy still assigns adapter construction/DI to `apps/cli/`, but this repository's architecture places wiring in `apps/cli-composition/` and keeps `apps/cli` to parse/dispatch/output/ExitCode mapping. As written, the `usecase bypass` exception can hide direct domain/infrastructure construction in CLI and overlaps with the `cli_composition` briefing. Reframe this briefing so CLI is thin and wiring stays reportable in the composition layer.

This policy assigns adapter construction/DI to `cli_composition`, then states that CLI is the only non-infrastructure place where `std::fs`, `std::env`, and process spawning are allowed. That makes boundary I/O needed by `apps/cli-composition/` look forbidden even though this split makes composition the wiring layer. Narrow this statement to `apps/cli/` command-handler responsibilities or explicitly describe the allowed boundary I/O for `cli_composition`.

The `What to report` list introduces reportable `cli_composition` categories without grounding most of them in the named conventions. The briefing requires each report category to cite its convention basis, but bullets such as port-adapter pairing, application logic in composition, leaked test fixtures, panic in wiring, and double-instantiation have no convention or architecture-rule citation. Add explicit citations or remove categories that are not convention-backed.

T001 is internally inconsistent: it says the step is "purely additive" and that "no existing files are modified", but the same step also requires moving `track/review-prompts/plan-artifacts.md` into `.harness/custom/review-prompts/`. A move changes the source tree, so this step cannot satisfy both statements as written.

T002 is declared CI-green at the commit boundary, but T003 is where the tests that still assert the old path strings are updated. If those tests are part of `cargo make ci`, the T002 boundary is not actually passable until the T003 expectation changes land too.

CN-01/ADR D5 require the clean-move migration commit to update all references, explicitly including docs, at the same time as loader paths, briefing_file values, harness-policy patterns, and tests. The implementation plan instead makes T002+T003 the atomic migration and then schedules the live-doc reference updates as T004 after that paired migration. That leaves an intermediate planned commit sequence that violates the stated clean-move constraint and contradicts the task coverage that maps CN-01 only to T002/T003 while IN-07/IN-08 are deferred to T004.

OS-01 says the broken `briefing_file` path lint is deferred per the ADR Open Questions, but its ADR reference points to D6. D6 defines `.harness/custom/` ownership and CI behavior, not the broken-path lint deferral, so the rendered spec sends readers to the wrong design rationale for this out-of-scope item.
